concepts.title: Forms of Straight Line
concepts.conceptBody:
Equation of line in different forms 

1. Slope-Intercept Form 

In this type you are provided with the intercept line makes with y-axis and also the slope of the line.

Then the equation is

$\text{y}=\text{mx}+\text{c}$

where $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }m=$ slope of the line $=\text{tan}\alpha $

$\text{c}=\text{y-intercept }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$

Note: If the line passes through origin i.e. $c=0$ then the equation of line becomes $\text{y}=\text{mx}$.

2. Point-Slope Form 

When a point on a line $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ and slope of the line is given to us, then the equation of the line becomes

$\text{y}-{{\text{y}}_{1}}=\text{m}\left( \text{x}-{{\text{x}}_{1}} \right)$

3. Two-Point Form 

(a) When two points lying on the line are given to us, then equation of line becomes

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & \left( y-{{y}_{1}} \right)=\left( \frac{{{y}_{2}}-{{y}_{1}}}{{{x}_{2}}-{{x}_{1}}} \right)\left( x-{{x}_{1}} \right),\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \\ {} & \left( y-{{y}_{2}} \right)=\left( \frac{{{y}_{2}}-{{y}_{1}}}{{{x}_{2}}-{{x}_{1}}} \right)\left( x-{{x}_{2}} \right) \\ \end{array}$

(b) Determinant Form 

Equation of line passing through points $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}}{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)\And \text{Q}\left( {{\text{x}}_{2}}{{\text{y}}_{2}} \right)$ can also be given as

$\left| \begin{matrix} x & y & 1 \\ {{x}_{1}} & {{y}_{1}} & 1 \\ {{x}_{2}} & {{y}_{2}} & 1 \\ \end{matrix} \right|=0$

4. Intercept Form

If a line $\text{L}$ cuts intercepts $\text{a}$ and $\text{b}$ at $\text{x}$-axis and $\text{y}$-axis respectively, then the equation of lines can be given as:

$\frac{\text{x}}{\text{a}}+\frac{\text{y}}{\text{b}}=1$

Note: 

If the line cuts the axes in negative part then the negative of a & b are taken accordingly like 
 

5. Normal Form

The equation to a straight line in terms of the perpendicular let fall upon it from the origin and the angle that this perpendicular makes with the axes of $\text{x}$.

Let $\text{OR}$ be the perpendicular from $\text{O}$ and let its length be $\text{p}$.

Let $\alpha $ be the angle that OR makes with OY.

Then the equation of line is $\text{xcos}\alpha +\text{ysin}\alpha =\text{p}$.

Note: 

1.If $\alpha =0$ then equation $\text{xcosa}+\text{ysin}\alpha =\text{p}$ becomes i.e.,

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=p$

2.If $\alpha =\frac{\pi }{2}$ then equation $x\text{cos}\alpha +y\text{sin}\alpha =p$ i.e.,

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }y=p$

3.If $\alpha =0,p=0$ then equation $x\text{cos}\alpha +y\text{sin}\alpha =p$ i.e.,

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=0$

4.If $\alpha =\frac{\pi }{2},p=0$ then equation $x\text{cos}\alpha +y\text{sin}\alpha =p$ becomes i.e.,

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=0$

6. Parametric Form

When the point $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ and slope $\left( \text{m}=\text{tan}\theta \right)$ of the line is given to you, then the equation of the line can also be written as

$\frac{\text{x}-{{\text{x}}_{1}}}{\text{cos}\theta }=\frac{\text{y}-{{\text{y}}_{1}}}{\text{sin}\theta }=\text{r}$

where $r$ is the distance $PQ$

Also

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & x={{x}_{1}}+r\text{cos}\theta \\ {} & y={{y}_{1}}+r\text{sin}\theta \\ \end{array}$

These equations are known as parametric form of line

Note: The value of $\text{r}$ can both be positive or negative.

  • For point above the given point $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)\text{r}$ is $+\text{ve}\And $ hence new point is $\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}}+\text{rcos}\theta \right.$, $\left. {{\text{y}}_{1}}+\text{rsin}\theta \right)$
  • for points below the given point $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)\text{r}$ is - ve $\And $ hence new point is $\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}}-\text{rcos}\theta \right.$, $\left. {{\text{y}}_{1}}-\text{rsin}\theta \right)$
